final_prompt.prompt:
Create a panoramic view of Tang Dynasty Chang'an city during the Lantern Festival, ten thousand lanterns illuminating the night sky, rendered in the meticulous gongbi heavy-color handscroll tradition.

Style/medium: gongbi heavy-color painting (工笔重彩), the continuous narrative rhythm of traditional Chinese handscrolls, with the refined mineral pigment depth of Tang court painting
Composition: sweeping horizontal scroll format, eye traveling through the city from one district to the next in a meditative journey, dense conversation between architecture and celebration
Subject: Tang Dynasty Chang'an cityscape during Lantern Festival, streets and palace complexes lined with countless glowing lanterns
Scene/backdrop: the vast Tang capital at night, city walls, palace gates, market districts, and the grand thoroughfares all participating in the festival of light
Lighting/mood: the sacred stillness of ten thousand lanterns holding back the darkness, warm reverence rising from the collective glow
Color palette: mineral pigment richness—vermilion, malachite green, azurite blue, gold leaf accents—warm honey light against cool night indigo
Materials/textures: silk ground with the smooth precision of gongbi brushwork, layered mineral pigments creating luminous depth, fine ink outlines holding form
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: Tang Dynasty architectural accuracy, continuous scroll narrative flow, festival atmosphere preserved
Avoid: modern lighting effects, Western perspective distortion, flat digital coloring without pigment depth
